name: Spaghetti with Shrimp and Eggplant
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and devein the shrimp, then set aside.
2. Trim the ends of the eggplant and peel it. Cut into 1/2 inch cubes.
3. Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a saucepan over medium heat. Add the garlic and cook, stirring, until fragrant but not browned.
4. Add the tomatoes, honey, pepper flakes, basil, salt, and pepper to the saucepan. Stir to combine, cover, and simmer for about 15 minutes.
5. Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large nonstick skillet over high heat. When very hot, add the eggplant cubes and season with salt and pepper. Cook, tossing occasionally, until nicely browned on all sides.
6. Drain the cooked eggplant and add it to the tomato sauce. Stir to combine, cover, and cook for an additional 15 minutes or until well blended with the sauce.
7. In the same skillet, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of oil over high heat. Add the shrimp, salt, and pepper. Cook for one minute, stirring occasionally, until pink and just cooked through.
8. Stir the cooked shrimp into the tomato-eggplant mixture. Cook for an additional minute to warm everything together.
9. Bring a large pot of water to a boil. Cook the spaghetti according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and return the pasta to the hot water, reserving some of the cooking liquid.
10. Add the cooked shrimp and eggplant mixture to the drained pasta. Toss well to combine, then serve immediately with grated parmesan cheese on top. Serves 4.
